data.title: Oracle falls on report co's data centers for OpenAI delayed to 2028
data.description:
** Shares of AI cloud firm Oracle fall 5.4% to $188.31

** ORCL pushes back completion dates for some of the data centers for OpenAI to 2028 from 2027, Bloomberg News reports citing people familiar with the matter

** The delays are largely due to labor and material shortages, the report says

** Up to last close, ORCL had gained ~19% YTD

data.title: Digital Lifestyle Expert Mario Armstrong and News Media Group, Inc. Wrap up the Holidays with a Ho-Ho-Holiday Gift Guide in 2025 on a Nationwide Satellite Media Tour
data.description:
Top Brands Participated in the Media Tour, Highlighting a Curated Collection of Holiday Must-Haves.

West Palm Beach, Florida--(Newsfile Corp. - December 12, 2025) - As the holidays wind down, award-winning Digital Lifestyle Expert Mario Armstrong delivered his final roundup of gifts to help shoppers complete their lists. This comprehensive segment provided much-needed "Ho-Ho-Ho" inspiration for finding the perfect last presents.

Mario Armstrong showcased diverse ideas, including collectibles from a popular book series, the latest phone plans for enhanced connectivity, fun action figure toys, imaginative arts and crafts supplies, and top-selling video games.

The nationwide media tour successfully guided consumers through the final push of holiday shopping, ensuring their gift-giving was wrapped up with ease and great ideas.

A recap of the roundup of products along with details can be found on IntheNews.TV.

Cannot view this video? Visit:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_ftkhf3oBrc

Brands that were featured in the SMT included:

Epic Cat Ninja Figurines. The world's leading digital reading platform for kids, has launched its first-ever Cat Ninja toy line, bringing the beloved hero from the page to playtime.

TextNow. Get free and flexible phone service all in one app with TextNow - the only free phone service provider with unlimited talk & text, free data for select apps, and nationwide 5G coverage.

WWE® Money in the Bank® Cash-In Ring Playset. WWE fans can bring the excitement home with the WWE® Money in the Bank® Cash-In Ring Playset from Mattel, featuring lights, sounds, motion, and two thrilling modes that let the ring decide the fate of their favorite Superstars!

Crayola Marker Airbrush Kit. The ultimate holiday gift for kids who want to step into the world of spray art magic and get creative in a brand-new way!

Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds. The latest addition to the longstanding Sonic Racing franchise, allowing players to race across land, sea, air, and space while warping across new dimensions.

Two Point Museum. Aspiring curators are responsible for building incredible museums where they will design and refine the layout, keep staff happy, guests entertained, donations plentiful... and children off the exhibits.

All the details and links can be found on IntheNews.TV.

Media Contact Details:

Jennifer Rivera

President

News Media Group, Inc.

info@newsmg.com

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/277858

data.title: Germany – EDF wins Amprion BESS
data.description:
EDF Power Solutions has won a decentralised Grid Booster project with a total capacity of 250MW as part of a flexibility tender by German electricity transmission system operator Amprion.

The project comprises five batteries, each with a capacity of 50MW, making it one of the largest storage systems in Germany and EDF’s first grid-scale battery project in Germany.

The batteries will be installed at key nodes to optimise electricity flow. When not in use to stabilise the transmission grid, the system can be used to relieve the regional power grid.

EDF said the Grid Booster project is intended to support the transmission of electricity from the northern to the southern parts of the country, relieving grid imbalances and congestion. EDF Power Solutions will be responsible for operation and maintenance of the batteries throughout the project’s lifespan. Amprion will handle management in winter and EDF in summer.

EDF Power Solutions, a division of French utility EDF, was formed in 2025 through the combination of EDF Renewables and the EDF Group’s internal division. It has a portfolio of 800MW of battery storage systems in operation and 1.2GW under construction.

data.title: U.S. Stocks Move Mostly Lower Amid Tech Sell-Off
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Stocks have moved mostly lower over the course of the trading session on Friday, with the major averages all moving to the downside following the mixed performance seen in the previous session.

The tech-heavy Nasdaq has led the way lower, tumbling 396.53 points or 1.7 percent to 23,197.33, while the S&P 500 is down 76.56 points or 1.1 percent at 6,824.44.

The narrower Dow is posting a more modest loss after reaching a new record intraday high in early trading, falling 189.50 points or 0.4 percent to 48,514.51.

The weakness that has emerged on Wall Street comes amid a sell-off by tech stocks, as reflected by the slump by the Nasdaq.

Broadcom (AVGO) has helped lead the tech sector lower, with the chipmaker plunging by more than 10 percent on the day.

The steep drop by Broadcom comes even though the company reported better than expected fiscal fourth quarter results and providing upbeat guidance for the current quarter.

Tech stocks like Oracle (ORCL), Micron Technology (MU) and Advance Micro Devices (AMD) have also moved sharply lower, reflecting a continued rotation out of tech stocks.

Negative sentiment may also have been generated in reaction to comments by Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee explaining his decision to vote against cutting interest rates at last week's Fed meeting.

In a post on the Chicago Fed website, Goolsbee said he is uneasy about 'too heavily front-loading rate cuts and just assuming that inflation will be transitory.'

'While I voted to lower rates at the September and October meetings, I believe we should have waited to get more data, especially about inflation, before lowering rates further,' Goolsbee said.

Sector News

Computer hardware stocks are turning in some of the market's worst performances on the day, resulting in a 4.9 percent nosedive by the NYSE Arca Computer Hardware Index.

Networking, semiconductor and software stocks are also seeing substantial weakness, contributing to the steep drop by the Nasdaq.

Outside the tech sector, oil service, steel and gold stocks are seeing notable weakness, while airline stocks are bucking the downtrend.

Other Markets

In overseas trading, stock markets across the Asia-Pacific region moved notably higher during trading on Friday. Japan's Nikkei 225 Index jumped by 1.4 percent, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index surged by 1.8 percent.

Meanwhile, the major European markets have turned mixed on the day. While the French CAC 40 Index is up by 0.4 percent, the German DAX Index is just below the unchanged line and the U.K.'s FTSE 100 Index is down by 0.3 percent.

In the bond market, treasuries are giving back ground after moving higher over the two previous sessions. Subsequently, the yield on the benchmark ten-year note, which moves opposite of its price, is up by 5.1 basis points at 4.192 percent.

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Rivian Automotive Shares Gain on Plans for AI Autonomous Driving Features
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Rivian Automotive advanced on the electric-vehicle manufacturer's plan to roll out new autonomous driving features powered by artificial intelligence.

The stock was up 15% to $18.92 after hitting a 52-week high of $19.60 earlier in the Friday session. Shares are up 45% this year.

The company, which has consistently operated at a loss, is looking to Tesla for inspiration for the AI-centric approach, The Wall Street Journal reported. Rivian, like Tesla, has developed its own computer chip to quickly process data from the road to train the company's AI model.

New models will reach point-to-point hands free navigation next year, and the company expects its hands-free driving feature will expand this month to more than 3.5 million miles of roads in North America.

Rivian said it will begin charging for hands-free driving features starting in March.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: The Week in Oil: Oversupply, Ukraine Talks Drag Down Prices
data.description:
By Giulia Petroni

Here's a look at what happened in oil markets in the week of Dec. 8-12 and what the focus will be in the days to come.

OVERVIEW: Oil prices are on track for a significant weekly loss as an overall bearish supply outlook offsets persistent geopolitical concerns. In afternoon trading in Europe, Brent crude was just under $60 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate was at $57 a barrel. The benchmarks were down more than 4% on the week.

MACRO: Energy prices got a boost from the Federal Reserve's decision to cut interest rates this week, even though it was already largely priced in by investors amid widespread concerns about labor market weakness. A lower-interest-rate environment typically boosts economic activity, supporting demand for commodities.

Traders now hope for further easing next year.

"Although there are signs of a pause at the next meeting in January, the door remains open for further interest rate cuts after that," analysts at Commerzbank said. "We expect more significant interest-rate cuts than the market, especially after Powell's successor as Fed chair takes office in May."

GEOPOLITICAL RISKS: Geopolitics dominated market sentiment this week as traders closely monitored diplomatic efforts to end the war in Ukraine and with tensions escalating between the U.S. and Venezuela.

A Ukraine-Russia deal would likely prompt the U.S. to lift sanctions on Russian oil, removing a key market concern and easing the geopolitical risk premium. Meanwhile, the U.S. announced new sanctions related to Venezuela and seized a tanker off the country's coast, raising supply risks in the region and limiting further price losses.

SUPPLY AND DEMAND: The prospect of excess supply continues to hang over the market heading into 2026, with the key question now centered on the scale of the potential oversupply.

Global oil inventories surged to four-year highs in October, while oil in transit or stored on water also saw a notable increase as sanctioned barrels struggled to find buyers.

However, Brent's forward curve isn't yet supporting the carry trade required to remove crude from the market and into profitable storage plays for some time, Saxo Bank analysts said.

The carry trade--which occurs when traders buy physical crude, store it, and sell it forward at a higher price for delivery in the future--is only attractive when the futures curve is in contango, meaning future prices are significantly higher than spot prices.

OPEC's supply and demand forecasts now point to a relatively balanced market next year, in a sharp reversal from outlooks earlier this year that indicated tighter conditions. The International Energy Agency's projected surplus has narrowed, but the agency said it still expects a large supply overhang to cloud the outlook.

This prospect has contributed to oil prices losing nearly 20% so far this year.

"The end of the year is approaching, and it is becoming apparent that most energy prices will end the year with significant discounts," Commerzbank analysts said, noting that a barrel of Brent crude oil currently costs more than $10 less than at the beginning of the year.

WHAT'S AHEAD: Traders will continue to watch developments in Venezuela and Ukraine closely, as both remain key wildcards with potential to disrupt global supply. Meanwhile, China's crude oil processing figures are set to be published early next week.

On the economic front, a slate of Federal Reserve officials are set to speak following the central bank's rate cut.

Investors will also be watching for the delayed November jobs report and CPI data for further cues on labor-market conditions and inflation trends. The University of Michigan is scheduled to release its final December consumer-sentiment reading on Friday.

Write to Giulia Petroni at giulia.petroni@wsj.com

data.title: Oracle pushes back several data centers for OpenAI to 2028, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Oracle has pushed back the completion dates for some of the data centers it is developing for OpenAI to 2028 from 2027, Bloomberg News reported on Friday citing people familiar with the work.

The company's shares were down nearly 5%.

The delays are largely due to labor and material shortages, according to the Bloomberg report.

Oracle did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

data.title: Puerto Rico – Energiza PPP expanded
data.description:
By Nic Stone

Consortium Energiza has secured approvals from Puerto Rico’s energy regulator PREB to amend power purchase and operating agreements signed in 2024 with public utility Prepa for its planned thermal power plant public-private partnership in San Juan.

Energiza was tapped for the design, development, financing, construction, installation, and operation and maintenance of the 478MW gas-fired combined-cycle power plant, which has been expanded to 528MW and switched to GE turbines from Mitsubishi. The scheme can also be converted to run on hydrogen.

The approvals relate to a new take-or-pay structure under which Prepa will not pay for fuel shortfalls caused by the seller or force majeure events, with reductions in fuel costs passed to Prepa. Energiza is formed by Tropigas, Cratos Project Services, Mitsubishi Power and NAES. Operations are scheduled to begin in 2028.

data.title: CAE Shares Rise on C$270M Contract With Royal Australian Air Force
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

CAE shares rose after the company said it has won a contract to deliver a new air-mission training system for the Royal Australian Air Force.

Shares traded 3.2% higher at 40.22 Canadian dollars on Friday morning.

The Canadian company that specializes in training, simulation and operation support for aviation, defense and healthcare said late Thursday that it was awarded a contract with the Commonwealth of Australia to deliver the future-air-mission training system under Project AIR5428 Phase 3.

The initial 10-year contract is valued at more that C$270 million (US$196.1 million).

As part of the contract, CAE will work with partners to provide modern training, bringing together classroom learning, training materials and simulated ground and air exercises to mirror challenges of real-world missions, it said.

The training system will be set up at RAAF Base East Sale in Victoria to prepare officers for key Air Force roles, while also offering advanced instructor training to improve teaching effectiveness, CAE said.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

Panoro Energy ASA

Oslo, 12 December 2025 - Panoro Energy ASA ("Panoro" or the "Company") (OSE: PEN) initiated a share buyback program on June 02, 2025, to repurchase up to NOK 100 million in value of the Company's common shares in open market transactions on the OSE

For the period from and including 08 December - 12 December 2025, Panoro purchased a total of 100,000 shares at an average price of NOK 20.1155 per share.

Contact information

For further information, please contact: investors@panoroenergy.com

About Panoro

Panoro Energy ASA is an independent exploration and production company based in London and listed on the main board of the Oslo Stock Exchange with the ticker PEN. Panoro holds production, exploration and development assets in Africa, namely interests in Block-G, Block S, Block EG-01 and Block EG-23 offshore Equatorial Guinea, the Dussafu Marin, Niosi Marin and Guduma Marin Licenses offshore southern Gabon, the TPS operated assets in Tunisia and onshore Exploration Right 376 in South Africa.

data.title: UK – Varco gets BESS equity
data.description:
By Rod Morrison

Adaptogen Capital and its developer Varco Energy have raised £40m for its next battery storage projects, half from its own fund and half from other investors.

The company has energised its 57MW/138MWh Sizing John and 57MW/138MWh Native River projects, which are backed by a £58m finance facility from NatWest.

The next schemes will be the 47.5MW/95MWh Sambar development scheme at Indian Queens in Cornwall that is due online in Q2 2026 and the 85MW/212.5MWh extension of Sizing John in the northwest, due online in Q3 2026. Next up will be the 100MW/250MWh Old Allen Road scheme near Bradford, West Yorkshire.

The company is using equity finance at present and has adopted a merchant route with no tolling or floor-type contracts. It is proposing a 275MW/3GWh scheme, Al Boum, under the long-duration tender process in Stockton-on-Tees.

data.title: Oracle pushes back several data centers for OpenAI to 2028 from 2027, Bloomberg News reports
data.description: Oracle has pushed back the completion dates for some of the data centers it is developing for OpenAI to 2028 from 2027, Bloomberg News reported on Friday citing people familiar with the work.
